## Completed: Formal Definitions

All core definitions are formalized in Lean 4:

| Definition | Lean Name | Line |
|-----------|-----------|------|
| Autoconvolution ratio $R(f) = \|f*f\|_\infty / (\int f)^2$ | `autoconvolution_ratio` | 40 |
| Autoconvolution constant $c = \inf R(f)$ | `autoconvolution_constant` | 49 |
| Discrete autoconvolution $\text{conv}[k] = \sum_{i+j=k} a_i a_j$ | `discrete_autoconvolution` | 55 |
| Test value $\text{TV}(\ell, s_{\text{lo}})$ | `test_value` | 61 |
| Max test value $b_{n,m}(c)$ | `max_test_value` | 71 |
| Composition (vector summing to $m$) | `is_composition` | 81 |
| Bin masses $\int_{\text{bin}_i} f$ | `bin_masses` | 89 |
| Canonical discretization (floor-rounding) | `canonical_discretization` | 96 |
| Contributing bins $\mathcal{B}(\ell, s_{\text{lo}})$ | `contributing_bins` | 109 |
| Cumulative distribution helper $D(k)$ | `canonical_cumulative_distribution` | 116 |

## Completed: Foundational Lemmas

These theorems are fully proved in Lean 4:

| # | Theorem | Lean Name | Line | Status |
|---|---------|-----------|------|--------|
| F1 | $D(i+1) - D(i)$ rewrite | `canonical_discretization_eq` | 126 | **PROVED** |
| F2 | $D(0) = 0$ | `canonical_cumulative_distribution_zero` | 141 | **PROVED** |
| F3 | $D(2n) = m$ (when total mass $\neq 0$) | `canonical_cumulative_distribution_2n` | 153 | **PROVED** |
| F4 | Bin masses $\geq 0$ for $f \geq 0$ | `bin_masses_nonneg` | 162 | **PROVED** |
| F5 | $\sum c_i = m$ (zero mass edge case) | `canonical_discretization_sum_zero_mass` | 165 | **PROVED** |
| F6 | Discretization $= D(i+1) - D(i)$ (alt form) | `canonical_discretization_eq_diff` | 173 | **PROVED** |
| F7 | Telescoping sum $\sum (f(i+1) - f(i)) = f(n) - f(0)$ | `sum_fin_telescope` | 179 | **PROVED** |
| F8 | $D$ is monotone for $f \geq 0$ | `canonical_cumulative_distribution_mono` | 184 | **PROVED** |

## What This Covers

The completed work establishes the **discretization framework**: definitions of all key objects and proofs that the canonical discretization is well-defined ($\sum c_i = m$, monotone cumulative distribution, nonneg bin masses).

**No main claim from `mathematical_claims.md` is fully proved.** The framework lemmas are prerequisites for Claims 1.1, 1.2, 3.1, and others, but the core theorems (test value is a lower bound, correction term validity, pruning rule soundness, cascade completeness) remain to be proved.
